# Project Status

_Updated by: Lead agent_ &nbsp;·&nbsp; _When: 2026-07-15 (planning pass 4)_

## Phase

**build** — Phase 1–6 (engine + UI scaffold) is fully built and closed out (#4–#30 all CLOSED
except #31/#38 below). Project is now in a **post-MVP feedback/hardening loop**: client is
playing the running simulation and filing bug reports / feature requests directly as issues
(#32–#44, with #32 CLOSED/karen-PASSED), and the team is also fixing its own dispatcher
infrastructure (#46–#54, see below).

## ⚠️ New this pass — starvation bug recurred against #34; hard-sequenced the whole queue behind #52/#54

Last pass's tactical fix (backlogging #33 behind #52) worked exactly as intended — the
dispatcher moved on and picked up #34 next. But #34 then failed karen (see verdict below) and,
being relabeled back to `agent-todo` with the same low number, became the new "oldest" issue —
**re-starving #52 all over again**. #52 (URGENT — uncommitted work at real risk of loss) has now
gone **9+ full lead passes with zero worker dispatch attempts**, purely because dispatcher.sh
always picks the oldest-numbered `agent-todo` issue (root cause tracked in #54) and something
low-numbered keeps failing karen and reclaiming that slot.

Whack-a-mole single-issue fixes clearly won't keep up. This pass I broke the deadlock directly:
**every currently-open `agent-todo` issue except #52, #53, #54 was moved to `agent-backlog`**
with `depends_on: #52, #54` added to each (#34, #35, #36, #39, #40, #41, #42, #43, #44, #46,
#47, #48, #49, #50 — 14 issues, each has an explanatory comment). This guarantees:
1. The **very next** worker slot goes to **#52** (only remaining low-numbered agent-todo issue) —
   finally unblocks the URGENT git cleanup.
2. After #52 closes, **#53** and then **#54** (the actual dispatcher fairness fix) get the next
   slots — #54 landing is what stops this pattern from recurring.
3. All 14 backlogged issues auto-promote back to `agent-todo` together once **both** #52 and #54
   are closed, at which point the fixed (non-starving) dispatcher takes over normal ordering.

This is a temporary queue-priority measure, not a claim that those 14 issues are blocked on
#52/#54 technically (most aren't) — it's explicit in each comment. Net effect: **only #52, #53,
#54 are `agent-todo` right now**; nothing else will dispatch until #52 and #54 both close.

## Prior-pass finding (superseded by the above, kept for history)

Moved **#33 to `agent-backlog`, `depends_on: #52`** last pass — this was a genuine technical
dependency (not just queue fairness): #33's last two karen FAILs both cited its diff being
entangled with #32's uncommitted files, which #52 exists to fix. Still in `agent-backlog`,
unaffected by this pass's change (already correctly gated on #52).

**#34 karen verdict (this pass, FAILED):** worker added `applyBiodiversityPressure()` (density-
dependent mortality for monoculture/overcrowding, tunable via constants.ts) — mechanism exists
and is real, but under a *reproducing* monoculture the fixed stochastic penalty can't keep pace:
a 40-creature single-species population grew unbounded to 13,021 in 50 ticks (26x the configured
500 cap), never diversifying or dying off. Also didn't write the required
`state/worker_output.txt`. Full verdict is on the #34 issue as a karen comment; next attempt
needs to scale mortality with overshoot (or throttle reproduction when overcrowded) instead of a
flat percentage.

The prior-pass finding about **no `git add`/`git commit` step anywhere in dispatcher.sh** (root
cause of the #32/#33/#34 entanglement in the first place) is still open, tracked by #52
(one-time cleanup) and #53 (root-cause fix going forward). Working tree is still dirty exactly
as described last pass — confirmed via `git status` this pass, now growing further (also
touches `engine.ts`, `constants.ts`, `App.tsx`, plus new untracked `monoculture.test.ts`,
`diag.test.ts` scratch file). #52 should finally get dispatched next pass.

## What this project is

Project Origins: a persistent ecosystem simulation where players guide life evolution through
species creation and environmental stewardship. MVP is a deterministic, local-first evolution
sandbox validating player attachment and emergent adaptation.

## Client decisions (all answered — #18 CLOSED)

| Q | Decision |
|---|----------|
| Q1 State mgmt | **Zustand** + separate event log for lineage/replay |
| Q2 Visualization | **Canvas 2D** for MVP |
| Q3 Energy budget | Radial solar dissipation from grid center (peak=10 at center) + **God Mode** control panel |
| Q4 First-play world | Pre-built barely-sustainable ecosystem spanning full ecological spectrum |

## Blockers

- **Dispatcher infra outage (2026-07-14 17:00 – 2026-07-15 02:00, #45)**: ~9.5hr near-total rc=1
  outage across worker/karen roles (lead partially functional). **Confirmed resolved** — 5+
  consecutive clean passes since 02:00. #45 closed. Root cause (OAuth expiry vs rate-limit vs
  credential-path difference) was never confirmed by the client and doesn't need to be — pattern
  fully recovered on its own.
- **Attempt-counter bug (#48, still open)**: `attempt_count` in dispatcher.sh counts worker-fail
  comments across an issue's *entire history*, never resetting on requeue. This caused #22, #23,
  #26, #31, #32, #33 to hit false `agent-blocked` states purely from the infra outage above, with
  zero real content-attempt failures. **This pass:** found `max_worker_attempts` had reverted to
  the default of 3 in `schedule.json` (an earlier stopgap bump to 10 never actually landed) — a
  requeue at exactly 3 lifetime attempts insta-blocks again with zero new dispatch under that
  default. Re-applied `max_worker_attempts: 10` in `schedule.json` (committed directly to main,
  operational/infra config) and manually relabeled **#32** `agent-blocked` → `agent-todo` with a
  comment explaining the requeue. Will revert the 3→10 bump once #48's proper self-resetting fix
  lands and closes.
